info.name: Web Sankul API
info.description:
Admin + customer endpoints for Web Sankul.

## Conventions
- Collection-level **Bearer auth** — every request inherits `{{accessToken}}` unless explicitly marked otherwise.
- **Auto-ID capture** — every `Create` endpoint has a test script that stashes the new `_id` into the matching collection variable (e.g. `{{course_id}}`, `{{plan_id}}`). Subsequent PUT / DELETE requests reference those variables directly.
- **Runnable flows** — the `Admin APIs → 00 Getting Started` folder is a left-to-right happy-path sequence you can run via *Runner* to seed a full course with masters, relations, and plans.

item.name: Customer APIs
item.description: Endpoints hit by the Mobile App / Student Web Portal.

request.description: On success, stashes `{{accessToken}}` + `{{refreshToken}}` + `{{user_id}}`.

exec: const uid = j?.data?.admin?.id || j?.data?.admin?._id || j?.data?.user?.id || j?.data?.user?._id || "";
const uid = j?.data?.admin?.id || j?.data?.admin?._id || j?.data?.user?.id || j?.data?.user?._id || "";

request.description: Invalidates all CustomerAccessToken records for the authenticated customer and clears the Redis session key.
Public endpoint — updates `firebaseToken` for the customer matched by `phoneNumber`. Called post-login from the device.
Soft-deletes the authenticated customer (`isAccountDeleted=true`, `status=false`), invalidates all tokens, and clears the Redis session.
Replaces the authenticated customer's selected goal-label IDs. Invalid ObjectIds are silently filtered out.
Returns all active goals; each label carries an `isSelected` flag based on the authenticated customer's selections.

item.name: 04 Address
item.description: Customer address book and public location/onboarding dropdowns. `states`, `districts`, `educations`, and `characteristic` are public; address CRUD requires auth.

request.description: Returns `{ educations, goals }` for onboarding screens. Public — no auth required.
Soft-deletes the address (sets `status=false`).

item.name: 05 Courses
item.description: Customer-side course endpoints. The 4 routes exposed by the Course Management port.

request.description:
Fetch an encrypted video for playback. For free videos no subscription check is performed. For paid videos an active subscription with endAt > now is required.

Query params:
- **id** — video ObjectId (required)
- **type** — `course` or `package` (required)
- **course** — courseId when type=course
- **package** — packageId when type=package

Returns course (populated subject + educator), lectures (each with encrypted `videoURL` + `token`), materials tree, exams tree, pricing plans (split by `withMaterial`), and public promo codes active right now. Fires a `VIEW_COURSE` CRM lead async.
Upserts a (CustomerAddress, CustomerShipping) pair for the current user. `alternate_phone`, `email`, `state` can be null. On read, `state` is populated and numeric fields are stringified.
Returns the subscription with `package`, `course`, `customerShipping` populated. If `trackingId` is set, adds `tracking_id` + `tracking_url` (MAHAVIR if trackingId < 119400228001, else TIRUPATI).
Streams `application/pdf`. Use Postman *Send and Download*.

item.name: 06 Referral
item.description: Referral program — rewards overview, code generation, ledger, withdrawals, and bank accounts. All endpoints require customer auth.

request.description: Returns `{ customer, program }`. `program` is the active `student` referral program config.
Paginated referral ledger. Optional `type=credit|debit` filter.
Customer-picked code. Regex: `/^[A-Z0-9]{8,10}$/`. One-shot — can't be regenerated. Blacklist is enforced.
Minimum ₹500. Amount must be ≤ `customer.rewardPoints`. Creates a `pending` debit txn; admin must mark it `successful` after payout.

item.name: 07 Books
item.description: Physical book catalogue, cart, shipping, and orders. Browse is open; cart/shipping/order endpoints require customer auth.

request.description: Returns `{ cartId, books[] }`. Each book carries `qty` (quantity already in the customer's active cart) and `key` (`combo`|`individual`). If unauthenticated, `qty` is always 0.
Returns `{ cartId, items[], shipping, totals }`. `totals.shippingWaived` reflects the free-shipping threshold (from `BookSetting`).
Upserts a line in the customer's active cart. If the book already exists in the cart, the qty is overwritten with the new value.
Soft-deactivates the cart (`status=false`). A new active cart is auto-created on the next add-to-cart call.
Upserts a CustomerShipping row (matched on name+phone+address+pincode for the same customer) and attaches its `_id` to the active cart.

Cart must be non-empty and have a shipping address attached. Totals are recomputed server-side from the books' current prices + the free-shipping setting. Creates a BookOrder in `pending` status. A free/₹0 order is auto-marked `verified` and the cart is closed.
Each order is decorated with `trackingUrl` when courier+trackingId are set.

item.name: 08 Exams
item.description: MCQ exam module. Customer flow: browse categories → pick exam → start attempt → autosave → submit → view solution/analytics. Server owns the timer via `deadlineAt`. The correct answer is NEVER leaked before submission.

request.description: Get exam metadata (title, duration, marks, instructions) before starting.
Returns all questions with shuffled options (no correct answer exposed).
Submit all answers. timing format: MM:SS or HH:MM:SS. ratting is optional (1-5).

item.name: 09 Materials
item.description: Study-material browsing. Top-level entry points are exposed on the course/package detail pages via `materialCategories[]`. This folder drills down the tree and returns leaf PDFs.

request.description:
Returns `{ current, breadcrumbs, subjects, materials }`.

- `subjects[]` — child categories, each decorated with `havingChildDirectory`, `count` (recursive leaf count), and `isNewlyAdded` (materials added in the last 10 days).
- `materials[]` — leaf PDFs at this node.
- `breadcrumbs[]` — ancestor chain ending with the current category.

Client walks the tree by calling this endpoint recursively: if a subject has `havingChildDirectory=true`, tap it to drill deeper; otherwise its leaves will be in `materials[]`.
Deep-link support: single material record with populated category.
Increments `downloadCount`. Fire-and-forget — the client opens the `file` URL after (or before) calling this.
Latest materials across all active categories within the past N days (default 10).

item.name: 10 Packages
item.description: Package = parent bundle of video subjects, materials, exams, and pricing plans. Client discovery + detail + my-subscriptions + subscription-gated chat.

request.description: Packages under a given PackageType with plans (split `withMaterial`/`withoutMaterial`) + `subscriberCount`.
Subscriptions whose `packageId` is set and `endAt > now`.
Returns `{ package, subjects, materials, exams, plans: { withMaterial, withoutMaterial }, availablePromoCode }`. Each subject/material/exam carries `havingChildDirectory` for drill-down.
Returns admin-posted messages for this package. Requires an active subscription — returns 403 otherwise.
Returns the most recent messages for a live class (newest-first). Use `before` (ISO date) for cursor-based pagination to load older messages.
Returns the currently active poll for a live class (if any), plus `myVote` (option index) if the customer already voted. Returns 204 when no active poll.

Returns `{ title, streamId, status, isLive, hlsUrl, hlsUrls, recordings, liveClassId }`. While the stream is live, open `hlsUrl` in an HLS player. After Streamos posts to the recording webhook, `status` becomes `READY` and `recordings[]` is populated. `liveClassId` (stringified `streamId`) is the room id to use when joining the Socket.IO room for chat/polls.

Public endpoint Streamos calls after a stream ends and the recordings are ready. Updates the matching `LiveSession` with `recordings[]` and flips `status` to `READY`. Not called by app clients — included here for inspection/testing.

## Socket.IO — Live Chat & Poll

**Connection**: `ws://<host>/socket.io`  
**Auth**: send `token` in `handshake.auth` (customer JWT)

---

### Client → Server events

| Event | Payload | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `join_live_chat` | `{liveClassId}` | Join the room; receives `chat_history` + `active_poll` |
| `send_message` | `{liveClassId, message}` | Send a chat message (max 2000 chars) |
| `submit_vote` | `{pollId, optionIndex}` | Cast a vote on the active poll (one vote per user) |
| `leave_live_chat` | `{liveClassId}` | Leave the room |

---

### Server → Client events

| Event | Payload | Description |
|-------|---------|-------------|
| `chat_history` | `{liveClassId, messages[]}` | Sent on join; last 50 messages |
| `new_message` | `{_id, liveClassId, customerId, userName, message, createdAt}` | New chat message broadcast |
| `active_poll` | `{poll, myVote}` | Sent on join if a poll is active; `myVote` = option index or null |
| `poll_created` | `{poll}` | Admin launched a new poll |
| `poll_update` | `{pollId, options[], totalVotes}` | Live vote counts after each vote |
| `poll_updated` | `{poll}` | Admin edited the poll (question/options changed) |
| `poll_closed` | `{pollId}` | Admin closed the poll |
| `poll_deleted` | `{pollId}` | Admin deleted the poll |
| `live_session_ended` | `{streamId, liveClassId, status: "ENDED", endedAt}` | Admin ended the live session. Client should close the HLS player, disable chat/poll UI, and leave the room. Further `send_message` / `submit_vote` events will be rejected by the server. |
| `recordings_ready` | `{streamId, liveClassId, status: "READY", recordings[]}` | Streamos has finished processing recordings and the server saved them. Client can swap the 'ended' UI for a recordings/replay list without polling. |
| `error` | `{message}` | Error from server |

item.name: 12 Live Courses
item.description:
Customer-facing live courses, organised folder-wise:
- **01 Browse** — list / detail (header stats + plan discount) / my subscriptions
- **02 Live Sessions** — watch live, with the server-enforced per-viewer preview
- **03 Recorded Lectures** — course-wise recordings + gated single-lecture playback
- **04 Purchase** — create the Razorpay order
- **05 Schedule** — the study timetable + timetable files
- **06 Reminders** — per-session 'remind me' for upcoming SCHEDULED classes

**Entitlement:** a non-subscriber gets a server-enforced 3-minute per-viewer preview of a live session; after that, playback URLs are withheld and `purchaseOptions` lists every attached course + plan. Buying ANY attached course unlocks the session.

item.name: 01 Browse

request.description: Returns `{ liveCourse, plans, subscribed }`. `subscribed` is true when the current customer holds an active verified subscription to this course.
The customer's own live course subscriptions. `status` = active | expired | all (default all). Each row carries the live course, plan, start/end window and a computed `active` flag.

All upcoming SCHEDULED live sessions across every live course the customer currently has an active verified subscription to. Sorted ascending by `scheduledAt` so the nearest-to-start session is first. Pagination via `page` and `limit` (default 50, max 100). Each row carries the session, its educator, and the source `liveCourses` (with `name`+`image`).

Discovery feed: every upcoming SCHEDULED live session across every active live course on the platform — visible to non-purchasers too. Sorted ascending by `scheduledAt`. Each row carries a `subscribed` flag indicating whether the current customer already owns access to at least one of the session's courses; non-subscribers can still tap a row to open the session detail endpoint, which serves a 3-minute free preview and then surfaces the purchase popup.

Discovery feed: every session currently airing (status `CREATED`) across every active live course on the platform — visible to non-purchasers too. Each row carries a `subscribed` flag; non-subscribers tapping a row open `/api/v1/client/live-sessions/:id`, which serves a 3-minute free preview and then surfaces the purchase popup.

item.description: Discover live courses and see what the customer already owns.

item.name: 02 Live Sessions

query.description: true → SCHEDULED with scheduledAt >= now, sorted ascending by scheduledAt (nearest-to-start at top).

request.description:
Metadata-only list of a live course's sessions.

**Ordering:**
- `upcoming=true` → ascending `scheduledAt` (the next-to-start session is on top).
- otherwise → future sessions first, nearest-to-start at the top, then past sessions most-recent-first. Sessions with no `scheduledAt` sink to the bottom. Opening the list always lands on "what's next".

Playback URLs / recordings are NOT included — use `GET /client/live-sessions/:id` for those (entitlement-gated). Each row carries a `canJoin` flag (true only while `status === "CREATED"`) and a `hasRecordings` flag.

Playback info for one session, with the entitlement gate applied:
- `accessLevel`: `full` (subscriber or open session) | `preview` (inside the per-viewer trial) | `preview_ended` (trial elapsed).
- `previewSeconds` (180), `previewExpiresAt`, `previewSecondsRemaining`.
- `hlsUrl` / `hlsUrls` / `recordings` are returned ONLY for `full` and `preview`. Once `preview_ended` they are withheld — the 3-minute cutoff is enforced **server-side**, per viewer, not client-trusted.
- `purchaseOptions[]`: every attached live course + its plans, so the user can buy ANY one of them to unlock. Sent during `preview` too, so the popup is ready the moment the timer hits zero.

`:id` accepts the Mongo session `_id` or the numeric `streamId`.

item.description: Watch a live class. Non-subscribers get a server-enforced 3-minute per-viewer preview, then a purchase popup.

item.name: 03 Recorded Lectures

request.description: The flat list of recorded live classes for the course — every ENDED/READY live session that carries Streamos-delivered recordings. Surfaces the raw Streamos recording straight off the session, so a class shows up even before an admin files it into a folder. Metadata only (title, date, available `qualities`, `recordingCount`, `locked`) — the mp4 URLs are NOT here. To watch one, open `02 Live Sessions → Get Live Session` with the item's `sessionId`; that endpoint applies the per-viewer preview / subscription gate. Non-subscribers also get `purchaseOptions`.

Recorded lectures grouped by folder — Videos an admin promoted into VideoCategory folders (plus any manually added videos). Distinct from the Streamos session-recordings list above. The folder/lecture structure is always returned; a lecture's `videoUrl` is included only for subscribers (or free lectures) — locked lectures carry `locked: true`. Non-subscribers also get `purchaseOptions`.

Gated single-lecture playback for a folder video. Verifies the video belongs to a folder of this live course, then requires an active subscription unless the lecture is free. On 403 the response `data` carries `purchaseOptions`.

item.description:
Recorded lectures, two ways:
- **Streamos session recordings** — the raw recordings off each past live session (use the session-recordings list, then open the session to watch).
- **Folder lectures** — Videos an admin promoted into folders (use the folder recordings list + Get Lecture).

item.name: 04 Purchase

request.description: Preview-only: validates a promo code against a plan and returns `{ originalAmount, discountAmount, finalAmount, discountType, discountValue, ... }` so the UI can show the discounted total before checkout. The discount is always re-validated inside create-order — this result is never trusted on its own. Live courses use the promo-level discount on the PromoCode (discountType + discountValue); any active code within its window applies.

Creates a pending `LiveCourseSubscription` + a Razorpay order. `promocode` is OPTIONAL — when present, a promo-level discount is applied and the order is created for the reduced amount (always re-validated server-side; a code that drops the price below ₹1 is rejected). After Razorpay checkout, POST the returned ids to `Customer → Payment → Verify` — the live course branch flips the subscription to verified and computes start/end from the plan duration. The Razorpay webhook also fulfils it as a safety net. Returns 409 if the customer already has an active subscription to this live course.

item.description: Buy a live course, optionally with a promo code. After Razorpay checkout, POST the ids to `Customer → Payment → Verify` — the live course branch flips the subscription to verified. The Razorpay webhook also fulfils it as a safety net.

item.name: 05 Schedule

query.description: true → only classes with scheduledAt >= now, sorted ascending (nearest-to-start at top).

request.description:
The Schedule tab. Returns `timetable` (rows derived from the course's scheduled live sessions — subject, educator, date, start/end time) and `files` (the uploaded 'Time Table' PDFs). Not entitlement-gated — it's course info shown to everyone.

**Ordering:**
- `upcoming=true` → ascending `scheduledAt` (the next-to-start class is on top); the timetable is limited to classes from now onward.
- otherwise → future classes first, nearest-to-start at the top, then past classes most-recent-first. Opening the timetable always lands on "what's next".

item.description: The course's study timetable, derived from its scheduled live sessions, plus the uploaded timetable files.

item.name: 06 Reminders

request.description:
Set (or replace) the caller's reminder for a SCHEDULED live session. `minutesBefore` is optional — default 30, max 10080 (1 week). The reminder push fires that many minutes before the session's `scheduledAt`; if the class is sooner than `minutesBefore`, it fires almost immediately instead of in the past.

Upserts on (customer, session) — calling it again just moves the fire time. Returns the reminder with its `session` summary populated. Errors: 404 session not found, 409 session is not SCHEDULED / has no upcoming time, 422 bad `liveSessionId` or `minutesBefore`.

query.description: Optional. Positive integer, max 100. With `upcoming=true` the default cap is 50; omit on the full-history view for no cap. Use `limit=2` for the home-screen "next 2 classes" card.

request.description:
The caller's reminders, soonest first. With `upcoming=true` the list is sorted by `session.scheduledAt` ascending (the class that starts next is on top) and keeps only still-scheduled reminders whose session start is in the future; without it the list is sorted by `remindAt` and includes fired / cancelled ones.

`limit=N` caps the response after sort/filter (positive integer, max 100). When `upcoming=true` and no `limit` is sent, the response is capped at 50. The response carries `total` (count BEFORE the limit, for "+N more" labels) and `limit` (the applied cap, or `null`). 422 if `limit` is not a positive number.

Each row carries the populated `session` summary and a derived `fired` flag (the scheduled fire time has already passed).
Whether the caller already has a reminder on this session — drives the per-session 'reminder on / off' toggle. `data.reminder` is the reminder object, or `null` when none is set.
Remove the caller's reminder for a session and cancel its pending notification. Note the path id is the **liveSessionId**, not the reminder id. 404 if no reminder was set.

item.description: Per-session 'remind me' for upcoming SCHEDULED live classes. A reminder is delivered as a push notification (FCM, `type: "live_reminder"`) `minutesBefore` the class starts — default 30 min. One reminder per customer per session.

item.name: Admin APIs
item.description: Endpoints hit by the Admin React Dashboard.

item.name: 00 Getting Started (Runnable Flow)
item.description:
One-click happy path. Run this folder via Postman *Runner* to: log in → seed masters → create a course → attach plans → verify. Each step writes its new `_id` into a collection variable so the next step picks it up automatically.

**Prerequisites:** your `.env` admin exists (Test@123 by default).

After the run:
- `{{educator_id}}`, `{{subject_category_id}}`, `{{material_master_id}}`, `{{video_category_id}}`, `{{child_video_category_id}}`, `{{video_relation_id}}`, `{{course_id}}`, `{{plan_id}}` are populated.

exec: const uid = j?.data?.admin?.id || j?.data?.admin?._id || j?.data?.user?.id || j?.data?.user?._id || "";
const v = d._id || d.id || d.course?._id || d.course?.id;
const v = d._id || d.id || d.course?._id || d.course?.id;
const v = d._id || d.id || d.course?._id || d.course?.id;
const v = d._id || d.id || d.course?._id || d.course?.id;
const v = d._id || d.id || d.course?._id || d.course?.id;
const v = d._id || d.id || d.course?._id || d.course?.id;
const v = d._id || d.id || d.course?._id || d.course?.id;

request.description: Step 9 — add a paid video under the video category created in step 4.
Public **only for the very first admin**. After bootstrap, this endpoint requires a logged-in super_admin token.
Stashes `{{accessToken}}` + `{{refreshToken}}` + `{{user_id}}`.

exec: const uid = j?.data?.admin?.id || j?.data?.admin?._id || j?.data?.user?.id || j?.data?.user?._id || "";
const uid = j?.data?.admin?.id || j?.data?.admin?._id || j?.data?.user?.id || j?.data?.user?._id || "";
const v = d._id || d.id || d.course?._id || d.course?.id;

item.name: 03 Course Management
item.description:
Everything related to courses: master data, per-course hierarchy, the course itself, and its pricing plans.

### How to use
1. Run `00 Masters` CRUDs once to seed educators / subjects / materials / video-categories.
2. Build the per-course video tree with `01 Video Hierarchy`.
3. Create the course with `02 Courses → Create`.
4. Attach one or more `03 Pricing Plans`.

All `_id` values flow via collection variables — no copy-paste needed.

request.description: Returns `{ educators, subjectCategories, videoCategories, materials }` — the data that powers the Course **Create** form dropdowns in the admin UI.

item.name: 01 Masters
item.description: Global master tables (reused across every course).

request.description: Fetch all Educators.
Create a educator. On success, `{{educator_id}}` is auto-populated by the test script so subsequent PUT / DELETE requests work without any copy-paste.

exec: const v = d._id || d.id || d.course?._id || d.course?.id;

request.description: Update the educator whose id is in `{{educator_id}}`.
Remove the educator whose id is in `{{educator_id}}`.
Fetch all Subject Categories.
Create a subject categorie. On success, `{{subject_category_id}}` is auto-populated by the test script so subsequent PUT / DELETE requests work without any copy-paste.

exec: const v = d._id || d.id || d.course?._id || d.course?.id;

request.description: Update the subject categorie whose id is in `{{subject_category_id}}`.
Remove the subject categorie whose id is in `{{subject_category_id}}`.
Fetch all Materials.
Create a material. On success, `{{material_master_id}}` is auto-populated by the test script so subsequent PUT / DELETE requests work without any copy-paste.

exec: const v = d._id || d.id || d.course?._id || d.course?.id;

request.description: Update the material whose id is in `{{material_master_id}}`.
Remove the material whose id is in `{{material_master_id}}`.
Fetch all Video Categories (master).
Create a video categories (master). On success, `{{video_category_id}}` is auto-populated by the test script so subsequent PUT / DELETE requests work without any copy-paste.

exec: const v = d._id || d.id || d.course?._id || d.course?.id;

request.description: Update the video categories (master) whose id is in `{{video_category_id}}`.
Remove the video categories (master) whose id is in `{{video_category_id}}`.
Fetch all Materials (via /courses/materials alias).
Create a materials (via /courses/materials alias). On success, `{{material_master_id}}` is auto-populated by the test script so subsequent PUT / DELETE requests work without any copy-paste.

exec: const v = d._id || d.id || d.course?._id || d.course?.id;

request.description: Update the materials (via /courses/materials alias) whose id is in `{{material_master_id}}`.
Remove the materials (via /courses/materials alias) whose id is in `{{material_master_id}}`.

item.name: 02 Video Hierarchy (per-course)
item.description: Manage the **child** video folders that live under a course, plus the parent↔child relations that build the tree.

exec: const v = d._id || d.id || d.course?._id || d.course?.id;

request.description: Also cascades any `VideoCategoryRelation` row that points to this folder (returns `deletedRelations` in the response).

exec: const v = d._id || d.id || d.course?._id || d.course?.id;

request.description: Returns the course with `courseEducatorId`, `courseSubjectCategoryId`, `videoCategoryId`, `pcMaterialId` populated to `{_id, name|title}`, plus that course's pricing plans.

Creates the course **and** auto-creates a `{name} - Root` VideoCategory in the same transaction. Both `_id`s come back in `data.course._id` and `data.folder._id`; the test script stores the course id into `{{course_id}}`.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "GPSC Full Prep 2026",
  "description": "Comprehensive GPSC prep course.",
  "image": "https://example.com/img/gpsc.jpg",
  "ordered": 1,
  "shareableLink": "https://example.com/c/gpsc",
  "withMaterial": "yes",
  "withoutMaterial": "no",
  "level": "advanced",
  "status": true,
  "courseEducatorId": "{{educator_id}}",
  "courseSubjectCategoryId": "{{subject_category_id}}",
  "videoCategoryId": "{{video_category_id}}",
  "pcMaterialId": "{{material_master_id}}"
}

request.description: Transactional cascade — also deletes matching `PackageCourseEbookPrice`, `VideoCategory` (scoped by `courseId`), and any `VideoCategoryRelation` rows that reference those folders. Returns `deletedPlans` / `deletedCourseVideoCategories` / `deletedVideoRelations`.

item.name: 04 Pricing Plans
item.description: Plans are scoped under a specific course.

exec: const v = d._id || d.id || d.course?._id || d.course?.id;
const v = d._id || d.id || d.course?._id || d.course?.id;

item.name: 05 Videos
item.description: CRUD for individual videos within a video category. Create a video first — the test script auto-captures {{video_id}}.

request.description: Filter by videoCategoryId and/or status. Paginated.
Fetch a single video by ID.
Creates a new video under a video category. Captures {{video_id}} on success.
Bulk-update the order field for multiple videos in one call.
Partial update — only fields provided are changed.
Permanently deletes the video.

item.name: 07 Customer Management
item.description: Admin CRUD for customers — list, search, create, update, soft-delete, toggle status, and view per-customer subscriptions.

request.description: Returns all active states and education levels for use in create/edit forms.
Returns active districts for the given stateId.
Paginated list. Filter by search (name/phone/email), status, state, district, or registration date range.
Returns full customer profile with populated state, district, education, and subscription counts.
Creates a new customer. Auto-captures `{{user_id}}`.
Partial update — only provided fields are changed. phoneNumber cannot be changed here.
Flips the customer's active/inactive status. Returns the new status value.
Soft-deletes the customer (sets isAccountDeleted=true, status=false). Does not permanently remove the record.
Paginated list of this customer's course subscriptions with populated course and plan details.
Extends or adjusts the subscription end date.
Paginated list of this customer's ebook subscriptions. Each entry includes an `isActive` flag (status=true AND endAt > now).

item.name: 06 EBooks
item.description: Full EBook management — CRUD, pricing plans, and subscriptions. Run Create EBook first to capture {{ebook_id}}, then Create EBook Plan to capture {{ebook_plan_id}}.

request.description: Paginated list. Filter by search (name/author), author, publisher, language (English/Gujarati/Hindi), status.
Returns the ebook plus its active pricing plans.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "GPSC Complete Guide 2026",
  "description": "Comprehensive guide for GPSC Class 1 & 2 preparation.",
  "author": "Dr. Rajesh Patel",
  "publisher": "WebSankul Publications",
  "language": "Gujarati",
  "order": 1,
  "image": "https://cdn.example.com/ebooks/gpsc-guide.jpg",
  "thumbnail": "https://cdn.example.com/ebooks/gpsc-guide-thumb.jpg",
  "demoUrl": "https://cdn.example.com/ebooks/gpsc-demo.pdf",
  "bookUrl": "https://cdn.example.com/ebooks/gpsc-full.pdf",
  "link": "https://websankul.com/ebooks/gpsc-complete-guide",
  "termsAndConditions": "For personal use only. Redistribution prohibited.",
  "status": true
}

request.description: Creates a new ebook. The test script auto-captures `{{ebook_id}}`.
Partial update — only provided fields are changed.
Bulk-update the display order of ebooks.
Deletes the ebook and all its pricing plans in a single transaction.
Returns all pricing plans (active + inactive) for the ebook.
duration is in days. Auto-captures `{{ebook_plan_id}}`.
Partial update — only provided fields are changed.
Returns active pricing plans for an ebook — used when creating a manual subscription.
Filter by customerId, ebookId, status. Paginated.
Creates an EbookOrder + EbookSubscription atomically. Provide either `planId` (duration is read from the plan) or `durationInDays` for a custom period. Auto-captures `{{ebook_subscription_id}}`.
Confirms a pending order — sets status to complete and attaches Razorpay IDs.

item.name: 08 Referral Management
item.description: Admin tools for the referral program: program config, transaction ledger, withdrawal approvals, CSV export, and manual reward adjustments.

request.description: `name` is unique. `referralDiscount` and `referralReward` are percentages (0-100). `minimumPrice` is the plan-price floor for eligibility.
Ledger of all referral transactions. Filters: `customerId`, `type` (credit|debit), `status` (pending|successful), `fromDate`, `toDate`.
Marks a pending debit (withdrawal) as `successful`. Only DEBIT txns are accepted.
Only pending debit transactions can be rejected. Refunds the coins back to `Customer.rewardPoints` and deletes the transaction atomically.
Streams `WithdrawalRequests.csv`. Use Postman *Send and Download*.
Atomically credits or debits a customer's `rewardPoints` and records a `successful` ledger entry. `type`: `credit` | `debit`.

item.name: 09 Book Management
item.description: Admin tools for the physical book catalogue, orders, shipping tracking, and store settings.

body.raw:
{
  "name": "GPSC Prelim Mastery",
  "author": "WebSankul Team",
  "image": "",
  "thumbnail": "",
  "description": "Complete prelim-focused book for GPSC.",
  "demoUrl": "",
  "weight": 800,
  "pages": 600,
  "dynamicLink": "",
  "listPrice": 499,
  "discountedPrice": 399,
  "shippingPrice": 50,
  "orderBy": 1,
  "language": "Gujarati",
  "isMagazine": false,
  "isCombo": false,
  "publication": "WebSankul Publication",
  "deliveryEta": "5-7 days",
  "status": true
}

request.description: `discountedPrice` must be ≤ `listPrice`. Prices are integers (rupees, no paise).
Allowed values: `pending`, `verified`, `shipped`, `delivered`, `cancelled`, `failed`. Auto-stamps `paidAt`/`shippedAt`/`deliveredAt`/`cancelledAt` and pushes an entry to `tracking.history`.
Sets courier + tracking id, bumps order status to `shipped`, pushes a history entry. `courier`: `mahavir` | `tirupati`.
Upserts the singleton `BookSetting` (key=`default`). `freeShippingMinOrderAmount` controls the cart-level free-shipping threshold.

item.name: 10 Exam Management
item.description: Admin tools for exam categories, exams, questions, and per-exam submissions/analytics. All endpoints require admin or super_admin.

query.description: `root`|`null` for top-level or a parent ObjectId

request.description: Returns the full tree of active categories, nested.
Blocked if the category has sub-categories or exams attached.
`type`: `daily|subject|mock|weekly`. `status`: `draft|scheduled|published|archived`. `language`: `en|gu|hi|bilingual`. `difficulty`: `easy|medium|hard`.
Deletes the exam, all its questions, and all attempts in a single transaction.
`type`: `single` (exactly one correct option) | `multi` (one or more). Minimum 2 options. Optional `positiveMarksOverride` / `negativeMarksOverride` per question.
Submitted attempts, highest-score first. Populates customer info.

Returns `{ overall, perQuestion }`. `overall`: totalAttempts, uniqueCandidates, avg/max/min score, avgAccuracy. `perQuestion`: accuracy, correct/wrong/skipped counts sorted by lowest accuracy first (toughest first).
Marks the attempt as `abandoned` and zeroes out score/accuracy.
Get lifetime exam analytics for a specific customer.

item.name: 11 Material Management
item.description: Admin tools for study-material categories (tree) and leaf PDF files. Course/package attach lives on the existing course-management flows.

query.description: `root`|`null` for top-level, or a parent ObjectId

request.description: Blocked if the category has sub-categories or leaf materials.

body.raw:
{
  "title": "Chapter 1 - Gujarati Poetry Notes",
  "description": "Compact notes for Chapter 1.",
  "materialCategoryId": "{{material_category_id}}",
  "file": "https://cdn.example.com/uploads/materials/ch-1-notes.pdf",
  "directLink": "",
  "thumbnail": "",
  "fileSize": 1048576,
  "fileMime": "application/pdf",
  "language": "gu",
  "isPreview": false,
  "order": 1,
  "status": true
}

request.description: `file` = absolute CDN URL to the uploaded PDF. Upload the file to your storage (S3/Spaces) separately and pass the public URL here.

item.name: 12 Package Management
item.description: Admin tools for packages — parent bundle of video subjects, materials, exams, pricing plans, chat, and subscribers.

exec: try { const id = pm.response.json()?.data?._id; if (id) pm.collectionVariables.set('package_type_id', id); } catch (e) {}

request.description: Blocked if any package still uses this type.

body.raw:
{ "name": "GPSC Prelim Pack 2025", "description": "

Complete GPSC Prelim preparation with videos, notes, and daily tests.

", "image": "", "shareableLink": "", "withMaterialText": "Printed notes + online access", "withoutMaterialText": "Online-only access", "packageTypeId": "{{package_type_id}}", "goalId": "{{goal_id}}", "pcMaterialId": null, "educatorId": null, "specificSubjects": [ { "category": "{{video_category_id}}", "order": 1, "status": true } ], "materialCategories": [ { "category": "{{material_category_id}}", "order": 1, "status": true } ], "examCategories": [], "order": 1, "active": true }

request.description: Embeds `specificSubjects`, `materialCategories`, `examCategories` directly (mirror of `Course.materialCategories[]` pattern). `goalId` replaces the old misleading `exam_id`. `withMaterialText`/`withoutMaterialText` are marketing strings — the real with-material flag lives on each pricing plan.

exec: try { const id = pm.response.json()?.data?._id; if (id) pm.collectionVariables.set('package_id', id); } catch (e) {}

request.description: Blocked if package has any subscribers — archive (toggle active=false) instead. Cascades to chat and video-relation pivot; detaches plans.
Reassigns existing `PackageCourseEbookPrice` rows to this package (nulls out any prior course/ebook link). A plan can only belong to one entity at a time.
Soft-detach — sets plan `status=false` (does not delete the plan record).
All `PromotedPackageCourseEbook` rows whose `planId` belongs to this package.
Deactivates all existing relations for this package, then upserts the given relation ids as active. Controls which descendant video categories are included in the package.

BFS-walks the `VideoCategoryRelation` tree starting from `Package.specificSubjects`, then activates every descendant relation for this package. Replaces the old Laravel recursive `getChildIds()` function.
Provide `text`, `mediaUrl`, or both. FCM push is stubbed (returns `pushSent: false`); wire it when `firebase-admin` is integrated.

item.name: 13 Plan Management
item.description: Standalone pricing-plan admin. Plans live in `ws_package_course_ebook_prices` and are mutually exclusive: each plan is attached to exactly one Course, Package, or Ebook. Use this folder for cross-entity listing, bulk ops, default toggling, and cloning.

request.description: Returns the plan plus `promotedCount` (promo-code linkage count) and `subscriberCount`.
Exactly one of `courseId`, `packageId`, `ebookId` must be set. If `isDefault=true`, any other default plan on the same entity is automatically unmarked.

exec: try { const id = pm.response.json()?.data?._id; if (id) pm.collectionVariables.set('plan_id', id); } catch (e) {}

request.description: Entity attachment cannot be changed via PUT — use Clone Plan to move a plan.
Sets this plan as the default for its owning course/package/ebook and unmarks any other default on the same entity.
Clones the plan onto a different course/package/ebook. Provide exactly one of `targetCourseId` / `targetPackageId` / `targetEbookId`. Cloned plan starts with `isDefault=false`.
Blocked if any of the plans have active subscribers.
Blocked if the plan has subscribers; archive (toggle status) instead. Also removes any `PromotedPackageCourseEbook` linkages.
Creates a live poll for a class. Closes any currently active poll first. Broadcasts `poll_created` to all students in the room.
Returns paginated list of all polls (active + closed) for a given live class.
Returns poll details with per-option vote counts and total unique voter count.
Marks the poll as closed (isActive=false). Broadcasts `poll_closed` to all students in the room.
Updates question and/or options. Blocked server-side if poll is closed or has votes > 0. Broadcasts `poll_updated` to all students.
Deletes the poll and all associated votes. Broadcasts `poll_deleted` to students so they dismiss the poll card.

item.name: 15 Live Class — Sessions (Streamos)
item.description:
Wraps the Streamos live streaming API. Requires `STREAMOS_ACCESS_KEY` and `STREAMOS_ACCESS_SECRET` in the server env.

**Lifecycle**

- `SCHEDULED` — created in DB only, no Streamos call yet. Has `_id` and `scheduledAt` but no `streamId`. Can be edited / deleted / started.
- `CREATED` — Streamos stream exists, `streamId/rtmpUrl/hlsUrl` populated. Use `rtmpUrl` in OBS; share `streamId` with students. Must be ended (not deleted) while in this state.
- `ENDED` — admin called `/end`.
- `READY` — Streamos posted recordings to the webhook.

**Start rule**: a SCHEDULED session can only be started within 2 minutes of `scheduledAt`. Late starts (after `scheduledAt`) are always allowed — sessions never expire.

Most endpoints accept either the Mongo `_id` (`{{live_session_id}}`) or the numeric `streamId` (`{{live_session_stream_id}}`) as the path param.

request.description:
Immediate create — calls Streamos right away. Returns the persisted session with `status=CREATED`, `streamId`, `rtmpUrl`, `hlsUrl`. Omit `scheduledAt` (or pass a past date) for this mode.

Same optional fields as Schedule:
- `liveCourseIds: [...]` — attach to one or more live courses (multi-cohort).
- `recordingTargetFolderId` — auto-promote the recording into this folder when Streamos delivers it. Folder must belong to one of the `liveCourseIds`.

Schedule for later. Pass `scheduledAt` (ISO 8601) in the future. Optionally pass `liveCourseIds: [...]` to attach the session to **one or more** LiveCourses simultaneously (admin can run a single live class across multiple cohorts). `liveCourseId: "..."` is still accepted as a single-id shortcut.

Optionally pass `recordingTargetFolderId` (a VideoCategory `_id` that belongs to one of the `liveCourseIds`). When set, the recording webhook auto-creates a Video record in that folder once Streamos delivers recordings — the admin doesn't have to manually promote. Omit this field to leave the recording only on the Live tab and promote later via `Videos: Add from Live Recording`.

Streamos is **not** called yet — the session is stored with `status=SCHEDULED` and only `_id` + `scheduledAt` are populated. Admin must later call `Start Scheduled Session` within 2 minutes of `scheduledAt` (or anytime after) to actually create the Streamos stream.

query.description: true → only SCHEDULED with scheduledAt >= now

request.description: Paginated list. Sort: scheduledAt asc, createdAt desc.

Promotes a SCHEDULED session to CREATED by calling Streamos. Returns the updated session including `streamId`, `rtmpUrl`, `hlsUrl`. Allowed only when `now >= scheduledAt - 2min`; rejects with 409 + remaining seconds otherwise. Late starts (after `scheduledAt`) are always allowed.

Promote one of this session's Streamos recordings into ANY VideoCategory folder as a Video — the folder may belong to a live course OR a recorded course, so recordings can be filed wherever they are needed. Pick the recording by `recordingIndex` (0-based) or `quality`; omit both for the best quality. Idempotent per folder: re-promoting returns the existing Video. The created Video keeps a `liveSessionId` back-link.

`:id` accepts either the Mongo session `_id` or the numeric `streamId`.

Edit `title`, `scheduledAt`, `liveCourseIds`, and/or `recordingTargetFolderId` of a SCHEDULED session. Pass `liveCourseIds: []` to clear all course attachments, or `recordingTargetFolderId: null` to clear the auto-promote folder. Rejects with 409 if the session has already been started.
Delete a SCHEDULED / ENDED / READY session. Rejects with 409 for a currently-live `CREATED` session — end it first via `/end`.

Returns `{ session, isLive, hlsUrl, hlsUrls }`. Path param accepts either the Mongo `_id` (`{{live_session_id}}` — needed for SCHEDULED sessions) or the numeric `streamId` (`{{live_session_stream_id}}`). Streamos `streamDetails` is only consulted when status is CREATED.

Calls Streamos `endStream` and flips local status to `ENDED`. Broadcasts socket event `live_session_ended` to the room so connected students close their player. Streamos later POSTs recordings to `/api/v1/client/webhook/recording`.

Public endpoint Streamos calls after a stream ends and recordings are ready. Finds the LiveSession by `streamId`, sets `recordings[]` and flips status to `READY`, and broadcasts socket event `recordings_ready` to the room so students currently sitting on the 'ended' UI see the recordings appear without a refresh.

If this webhook is missed for any reason, `Get Live Session` will recover recordings by polling Streamos `streamDetails` on the next admin/student fetch.

Not called by clients in production — surfaced here so admins can simulate it during testing.
Wraps Streamos `uploadedVideoDetails`. Returns `{ recordingId, hlsUrl, status, title, dateAndTime, recordings[] }` for a single past recording id (from the Streamos dashboard's View Details modal).
Wraps Streamos `orgDetails`. Useful to confirm credentials and the `recordingWebhook` URL Streamos thinks it should POST to. `accessSecret` is filtered out of the response.
Wraps Streamos `updateWebhook`. Registers the URL Streamos will POST to once a recording finishes processing. Set this once per environment to `https://<your-host>/api/v1/client/webhook/recording`.

item.name: 16 Live Courses
item.description:
Admin management of live courses, organised folder-wise:
- **01 Courses** — the live course entity (CRUD + popular toggle + sessions list)
- **02 Plans** — pricing plans
- **03 Folders** — VideoCategory folders for recorded lectures
- **04 Videos** — recorded lectures inside a folder (manual or promoted from a recording)
- **05 Subscriptions** — subscriber list, free-grant, extend / revoke

item.name: 01 Courses

request.description: Multipart form. Upload `image` as a file (preferred), or pass an `image` URL string. Server validates `courseEducatorId` / `courseSubjectCategoryId` exist when provided, rejects duplicate names with `409`, and **auto-creates a root VideoCategory folder** for the course (its id is stored on `liveCourse.videoCategoryId` and is also returned in the response as `rootFolder`).

formdata.value: Live classes for GPSC prep — Mon/Wed/Fri 7pm.

request.description: Partial update — pass only the fields you want to change. `image` may be a new file upload or an existing URL string.
Refuses with `409` if any sessions are still attached. Cascades: removes all folders, videos, and parent/child relations under the course.
Replace the whole 'Time Table' file list shown on the Schedule tab. Upload the PDFs via the generic upload endpoint first, then send the resulting URLs here.

item.description: CRUD for the live course itself. Creating a course auto-creates its root VideoCategory folder. Course create/update also accepts `classType` (live | live_offline | offline).

item.name: 02 Plans

request.description: Create a pricing plan for this live course. `duration` is in months. If `isDefault` is true, any other default plan on this course is unset (only one default at a time).
Refuses with `409` if any verified subscriptions point at this plan. Toggle `status: false` instead to retire the plan without breaking existing access.

item.description: Pricing plans (duration in MONTHS + price). One plan per course can be `isDefault`.

item.name: 03 Folders

request.description: Returns `{ folders[], relations[] }`. `relations` are VideoCategoryRelation rows for any folder referenced by parent or child — enough for the UI to render a tree.
Creates a subfolder. If `parentFolderId` is provided, also inserts a VideoCategoryRelation row linking parent → new child. Defaults the folder image to the live course image when not provided.
Cascades — removes videos and parent/child relations referencing this folder. Refuses with `409` if you try to delete the root folder.

item.description: VideoCategory folders that hold the course's recorded lectures. The root folder cannot be deleted.

item.name: 04 Videos (in a folder)

request.description: Add any pre-recorded video. Set `platform` to `"youtube"` / `"aws"` / `"vimeo"` and supply the matching id field. `aws_id` may hold any externally-hosted MP4/HLS URL — the frontend just plays whatever URL it receives.

Promote a Streamos recording from a past LiveSession into a Video record in this folder. Pick by `recordingIndex` (default 0) or `quality` (e.g. `"720p"`). The recording URL is stored on the Video as `aws_id` with `platform="aws"`, which the player will load as a regular URL.
Update any subset of a video's fields. The update is scoped to this folder, so a videoId from another folder is rejected with 404.
Bulk-set the `order` of videos in this folder. Only ids that actually live in the folder are touched; stray ids are ignored. Response returns `{ matched, modified }`.

item.description: Recorded lectures inside a folder. A video may be added manually, or promoted from a past live session's Streamos recording.

item.name: 05 Subscriptions

request.description: List LiveCourseSubscription rows. All filters optional. `paymentStatus` = pending | verified | failed; `status` = true | false.
Same as above but pre-scoped to one live course via the path id.
Fetch a single subscription with customer / live course / plan populated.

Hand a customer an active, verified subscription with no payment (paidAmount = 0). Window comes from the plan's duration unless you pass `durationMonths`, `startAt`, or `endAt`. Returns 409 if the customer already has an active subscription to this course — extend it via the update endpoint instead.
Extend (`endAt`), revoke (`status: false`), or correct `paymentStatus`. At least one field required.
Hard delete — for test / erroneous rows. To revoke a real customer prefer Update with `status: false`, which keeps the audit trail.

item.description: Admin view of who is subscribed, plus the manual free-grant flow and extend / revoke controls.
